content:
**Indiana Pacers Capture First Eastern Conference Title in 25 Years, Outlast Celtics in Game 7 Classic**

June 5, 2025 – The Indiana Pacers are headed to the NBA Finals for the first time since 2000, after an electrifying Game 7 victory over the Boston Celtics capped a remarkable playoff run. Led by All-Star guard Tyrese Haliburton and the breakout play of Bennedict Mathurin, Indiana clinched the Eastern Conference crown with a 112-108 win at TD Garden, stunning a Celtics team that many had pegged as the title favorite.

This improbable run was fueled by Indiana’s relentless pace, depth, and defensive resilience. After dispatching the Miami Heat and New York Knicks in hard-fought series, the Pacers entered the conference finals as underdogs against Boston. The series quickly became a chess match between Pacers coach Rick Carlisle and Celtics’ Joe Mazzulla, but it was Indiana’s bench—especially sixth man Andrew Nembhard—who repeatedly swung momentum with timely threes and gritty defense.

The defining moment came in the closing minutes of Game 7, as Haliburton drained a deep three to put Indiana ahead for good. Myles Turner sealed the game with a crucial block on Jayson Tatum’s drive, sending the Indiana faithful into jubilation.

“This team showed the heart and unity we’ve been building for years,” Haliburton said post-game. “Nobody believed in us but ourselves.”

The Pacers’ victory signals a shift in the Eastern Conference hierarchy, and their Finals appearance marks a triumph for a young core that matured ahead of schedule. As Indiana prepares to face the Western Conference champion, fans and analysts alike are marveling at the Pacers’ blend of teamwork, tactical savvy, and sheer willpower.
